file_name: WOMENSGOLFUNLIMITEDINC_03_29_2000-EX-10.13-ENDORSEMENT AGREEMENT - Intellectual Property Rights                 Confidentiality and Non-Use Obligations Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby assigns and agrees to assign all Recipient's rights in any Intellectual Property to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient hereby assigns and agrees to assign all Recipient's rights in any Intellectual Property to the Company, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the initial, absolute assignment of Intellectual Property rights. By adding 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing,' the assignment becomes conditional. This creates a contradiction in section (f) because previously there wasn't any condition to the assignement, which leads to uncertainty whether the Intellectual property belongs to the company or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Recipient further acknowledges and agrees that all new discoveries, inventions, improvements, processes, formulae, designs, drawings, training materials, original works of authorship, photos, video tapes, electronic images, documentation, trademarks and copyrights (the "Intellectual Property"), that may be developed, conceived, or made by Recipient, alone or jointly with others during her work for the Company, shall be the exclusive property of the Company and shall be deemed a work for hire. Recipient hereby assigns and agrees to assign all Recipient's rights in any Intellectual Property to the Company, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties. Recipient hereby grants to the Company power of attorney for the purpose of assigning all Recipient's rights in Intellectual Property to the Company for the purposes of filings, registrations and other formalities deemed necessary by the Company to prosecute, protect, perfect or exploit its ownership and interests in Intellectual Property. Recipient further agrees to execute, acknowledge and deliver any documentation, instruments, specifications or disclosures necessary to assign, prosecute, protect, perfect or exploit the Company ownership of Intellectual Property.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient assigns all rights in Intellectual Property to the Company. The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties', which introduces a condition to the assignment. This contradicts the initial absolute assignment stated earlier in section (f), creating uncertainty about whether the Intellectual Property belongs to the company. The clause stating that the Intellectual Property shall be the 'exclusive property of the Company' is now conditional, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that she will not copy the Information in whole or in part or use all or any part of the Information to reverse engineer, duplicate the function, sequence or organization of the Information for any purpose without the prior written permission of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that she will not copy the Information in whole or in part or use all or any part of the Information to reverse engineer, duplicate the function, sequence or organization of the Information for any purpose without the prior written permission of the Company, except for internal training purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a strict restriction on copying or using the information without prior written permission. The addition of 'except for internal training purposes' introduces a direct contradiction. This creates uncertainty and in-text contradiction in section (e) regarding copying and use of the information, that causes uncertainty on how and for what reasons could information be copied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Recipient agrees that she will not copy the Information in whole or in part or use all or any part of the Information to reverse engineer, duplicate the function, sequence or organization of the Information for any purpose without the prior written permission of the Company, except for internal training purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits copying or using the information without prior written permission. The changed text introduces an exception ('except for internal training purposes'), directly contradicting the initial restriction. Since the Contradicted text (e) and the Changed text are identical, this demonstrates that the contradictory language is placed in the contract itself, leading to an in-text contradiction as the clause is effectively stating two opposing conditions. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability as it's unclear under what circumstances copying is permissible without prior approval.
